source.title: The gateway hypothesis of rostral prefrontal cortex (area 10) function

confidence_rationale: TiCS review synthesising a decade of fMRI and lesion evidence into a coherent gateway framing. Directly load-bearing for MECH-261: the proposed frontopolar function (switch between stimulus-oriented and stimulus-independent attending) is exactly what MECH-261's operating_mode vector formalises at a substrate level. High mapping fidelity, widely cited, still actively built on in current literature.
failure_signatures: If medial BA 10 had not shown preferential activity during stimulus-oriented attending vs lateral BA 10 during stimulus-independent attending, the anatomical refinement of the gateway would fail and the function would collapse into generic PFC activity.

If patients with BA 10 lesions showed no greater disruption of transitions between stimulus-oriented and stimulus-independent states than other frontal patients, the gateway framing would not be substrate-specific.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Rostral PFC (BA 10) supports a cognitive gateway that mediates the switch between stimulus-oriented attending (attention to environmental stimuli) and stimulus-independent attending (attention to self-generated or internally-maintained representations). Medial rostral PFC supports stimulus-oriented attending; lateral rostral PFC supports switching to and maintaining stimulus-independent cognition. The gateway function applies whenever an agent needs to hold one attentional mode to an unusual degree or switch between them.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the clearest biological analogue for MECH-261's operating_mode vector that exists in the literature. The gateway hypothesis partitions attending into stimulus-oriented (external_task mode) and stimulus-independent (internal_planning / internal_replay / offline_consolidation modes). Burgess's lateral rostral PFC is structurally the natural home for the switch-to-and-maintain-internal-mode function that MECH-261 gates writes around. This implies SD-033e is not merely about tree-search branching (Koechlin framing) but more fundamentally about switching between engagement with the external world and self-generated cognition. That is a broader and richer function than 'deliberative_branching' captures. Implications: (1) the MECH-261 mode vocabulary is biologically motivated at substrate level, not just operationally convenient; (2) SD-033e's V4 scope should include the gateway role explicitly; (3) the reserved V4 mode name may need revision (see proposed updates in synthesis.md Prong D).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The gateway hypothesis is still a theoretical framework, not a measured mechanism. The medial/lateral BA 10 dissociation it predicts has fMRI support but is sensitive to task design, and alternative framings exist (Mansouri's relative-importance-monitoring and Koechlin's branching being the main competitors). For REE, the gateway framing is useful because it is the most REE-compatible of the available accounts — but it is not the only credible one.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review in TiCS synthesising human fMRI and patient-lesion evidence on rostral PFC function. Draws primarily on Burgess-lab studies of prospective memory, self-generated cognition, and stimulus-independent attending.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (TiCS, Burgess lab, widely cited). Mapping fidelity very high — the gateway is essentially the substrate-level claim that MECH-261 makes at the operating-mode level. Transfer risk modest: human-only evidence but the target population for REE substrate claims is human/primate cognition.
